subject: Transfer Step 4 (engineer features) into combined notebook, preserving shared module import
description: Add a new markdown section '## Step 4: Engineer Features' after Step 3's cells, transferring engineer_csd_features() from pre_csd_4_engineer_features.py (02_thesis_data/_02_preprocessing/nielsen/CSD/pipeline_step_scripts/pre_csd_4_engineer_features.py, lines 116-159), which wraps the shared shared_engineer_features() (imported from _shared_modules/engineer_features.py, per pre_csd_4_engineer_features.py line 46). CRITICAL CONSTRAINT: do NOT inline or duplicate _shared_modules/engineer_features.py's logic into the notebook -- Danskvand/Energidrikke/RTD's own pipelines import this same module, and duplicating it here would immediately create the same drift risk this whole migration is meant to fix. Instead, add a notebook cell that does the equivalent sys.path.insert + import (mirroring lines 42-46 of pre_csd_4_engineer_features.py) so the notebook calls the real shared function, not a copy. Strip input_parquet_for_grain()/output_parquet_for_grain() (lines 105-115) and main()/argparse (lines 229+); keep only engineer_csd_features()'s wrapper logic (passing group_keys from GRAIN_CONFIG). Pass in-memory `df_filtered` from Task 4, producing `df_features`. Display the resulting feature columns (lag_*, rolling_mean_*, rolling_std_*, etc.) and confirm no leakage by checking lag_1 is NaN at each brand's first observation (the same spot-check used in P0027's prior verification). Verify by comparing df_features against a fresh `python pre_csd_4_engineer_features.py --grain bymonth` run's step_4 output parquet (same columns, same row count, matching non-null lag_1 pattern).
activeForm: Transferring Step 4 (engineer features) into combined notebook
